howItWorks.intro: This toilet water usage calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Daily Savings = (Old GPF - New GPF) x Flushes Per Day
Annual Cost Savings = (Annual Gallons / 1000) x Water Rate
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much water does an old toilet use?
faq.answer: Toilets made before 1994 use 3.5-7 gallons per flush. Models from 1994-2004 use 1.6 GPF. Current WaterSense certified toilets use 1.28 GPF or less. Dual-flush models use 0.8 GPF for liquid waste.

faq.question: Are low-flow toilets effective?
faq.answer: Modern 1.28 GPF toilets perform as well or better than old 3.5 GPF models thanks to improved engineering. Pressure-assist and gravity-fed designs ensure effective flushing with less water.
